item.title: Automating Large-Scale Analysis of Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts in Emerging Science and Technology
item.description:
Our study, “Automating Large-Scale Analysis of Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts in Emerging Science and Technology,” aims to understand and forecast the societal and economic impacts of emerging Scientific and Technological (S&T) advancements driven by Leading Organizations (LOs) and their Contributing Partners (CPs). By analyzing historical collaboration patterns and using machine learning, we will estimate the effort and investment needed to advance new technologies. Additionally, we will develop interdisciplinary, data-driven solutions to monitor Workforce Development (WFD) and Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Accessibility (DEIA) trends within S&T projects. This research received $300,000 in funding from NSF.

Funding Agency: NSF

Program: Division of Innovation and Technology Ecosystems (ITE)

Total Amount: $300K (My share: 79K)

Period: 09/2024 - 08/2026

Role: Co-PI

item.title: One project was funded by the National Science Foundation.
item.description: Our study, “Automating Large-Scale Analysis of Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts in Emerging Science and Technology,” aims to understand and forecast the societal and economic impacts of emerging Scientific and Technological (S&T) advancements driven by Leading Organizations (LOs) and their Contributing Partners (CPs). By analyzing historical collaboration patterns and using machine learning, we will estimate the effort and investment needed to advance new technologies. Additionally, we will develop interdisciplinary, data-driven solutions to monitor Workforce Development (WFD) and Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Accessibility (DEIA) trends within S&T projects. This research received $300,000 in funding from NSF.

item.title: One paper was accepted by ACL.
One paper was accepted by NAACL.
One paper was accepted by TKDD.
Abhishek Joshi
item.description: Master’s student in Computer Science at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi.

item.title: Amit Kumar
item.description: Master’s student in Computer Science at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi.

item.title: Bridging the Gap between Spatial and Spectral Domains: A Unified Framework for Graph Neural Network
Dynamic Multi-Context Attention Networks for Citation Forecasting of Scientific Publications
Patent Citation Dynamics Modeling via Multi-Attention Recurrent Networks
Crowdsourcing Cybersecurity: Cyber Attack Detection using Social Media
Determining Relative Airport Threats from News and Social Media
